flashcards.front: What does the 'Volume' characteristic of Big Data refer to?
flashcards.back: The enormous scale of data generated, measured in terabytes to zettabytes.

flashcards.front: Which V represents the speed of data generation/processing?
flashcards.back: Velocity - Refers to real-time data streams and rapid ingestion rates.
Diverse data types: structured (SQL), semi-structured (JSON), and unstructured (images).

flashcards.front: Define Veracity in Big Data context
flashcards.back: Data quality challenges including noise, inconsistency, and trustworthiness issues.

flashcards.front: Name 3 key technical challenges in Big Data
flashcards.back: 1. Storage scalability 2. Real-time processing 3. Data security/privacy
Distributed systems can only guarantee 2/3: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance.

flashcards.front: What does Hadoop solve in Big Data?
flashcards.back: Distributed storage (HDFS) and parallel processing (MapReduce) of large datasets.
Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple servers to handle large datasets.
Hybrid approach combining batch and stream processing for comprehensive analytics.
Uneven data distribution across nodes causing processing bottlenecks.
Decentralized ML approach where models train on local data subsets.

flashcards.front: What is the role of ZooKeeper in Hadoop?
flashcards.back: Centralized service for maintaining configuration and synchronization.
